2603.16557.title: BenchPreS: A Benchmark for Context-Aware Personalized Preference Selectivity of Persistent-Memory LLMs
2603.16557.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) increasingly store user preferences in persistent memory to support personalization across interactions. However, in third-party communication settings governed by social and institutional norms, some user preferences may be inappropriate to apply. We introduce BenchPreS, which evaluates whether memory-based user preferences are appropriately applied or suppressed across communication contexts. Using two complementary metrics, Misapplication Rate (MR) and Appropriate Application Rate (AAR), we find even frontier LLMs struggle to apply preferences in a context-sensitive manner. Models with stronger preference adherence exhibit higher rates of over-application, and neither reasoning capability nor prompt-based defenses fully resolve this issue. These results suggest current LLMs treat personalized preferences as globally enforceable rules rather than as context-dependent normative signals.
2603.16557.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地將用戶偏好存儲在持久記憶中，以支持跨互動的個性化。<lb/>然而，在受社會和制度規範約束的第三方通信環境中，一些用戶偏好可能不適合應用。<lb/>我們介紹了BenchPreS，該工具評估基於記憶的用戶偏好在不同通信情境中是否被適當應用或抑制。<lb/>通過使用兩個互補的指標，誤應用率（MR）和適當應用率（AAR），我們發現即使是前沿的LLMs也難以以情境敏感的方式應用偏好。<lb/>具有更強偏好遵循的模型顯示出更高的過度應用率，而推理能力和基於提示的防禦都未能完全解決這一問題。<lb/>這些結果表明，目前的LLMs將個性化偏好視為全球可強制執行的規則，而不是情境依賴的規範信號。
2603.16557.author: Sangyeon Yoon et.al.
2603.16557.authors: Sangyeon Yoon, Sunkyoung Kim, Hyesoo Hong, Wonje Jeung, Yongil Kim, Wooseok Seo, Heuiyeen Yeen, Albert No
